title: anchusa arvensis subsp. occidentalis
intro: Anchusa arvensis subsp. occidentalis, a subspecies of the wild alkanet or bugloss plant, is a flowering perennial native to western North America. It is valued for its bright blue flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its flavonoids and saponins content. Anchusa arvensis subsp. occidentalis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
